description: Obsidian Mnemosyne integration plugin files (docs + config) — 3 files

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: README documentation for the Mnemosyne Sync Obsidian plugin. Describes a plugin that exports Mnemosyne AI agent memories as markdown notes with YAML frontmatter into an Obsidian vault. Lists 6 features: memory sync, searchable notes, smart merge (won't overwrite edited notes), auto-sync (5-120 min interval), filtering (option to exclude assistant messages), and ribbon icon. Covers 2 installation methods (Obsidian Community Store coming soon, manual download from GitHub releases). Lists requirements: Mnemosyne installed (pip install mnemosyne-memory), Python 3.9+, desktop Obsidian. Defines 6 settings: data directory (~-relative path to Mnemosyne DB), memory bank selection, sync folder in vault, auto-sync toggle, sync interval in minutes, and include assistant toggle (default: user only). 43 lines.
nodes.languageNotes: Markdown documentation with a markdown table summarizing plugin settings. Includes bash commands for pip installation and descriptive lists for features. Makes no mention of API endpoints, authentication, or cloud services — purely local-first design.

nodes.name: manifest.json
nodes.summary: Obsidian plugin manifest defining the mnemosyne-sync plugin. Plugin ID: 'mnemosyne-sync', display name: 'Mnemosyne Sync', version 0.1.0, minimum Obsidian app version 1.5.0. Description: 'Browse, search, and manage Mnemosyne AI agent memories as markdown notes in your vault.' Author: Abdias J (github.com/AxDSan), desktop-only plugin, with a funding URL for GitHub Sponsors. 11 lines of JSON, minimal and standard Obsidian manifest format.
nodes.languageNotes: JSON following the Obsidian plugin manifest schema. Requires obsidian >=1.5.0, isDesktopOnly: true confirms it's a desktop-only plugin (no mobile support in v0.1.0).

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node.js package definition for the mnemosyne-sync Obsidian plugin build toolchain. Package name: 'mnemosyne-sync', version 0.1.0, MIT license. Main entry point: main.js. Two npm scripts: 'dev' (node esbuild.config.mjs) for development and 'build' (tsc -noEmit -skipLibCheck && node esbuild.config.mjs production) for production. Keywords: mnemosyne, memory, ai, agent, sync. Author: Abdias J. DevDependencies: @types/node ^20.0.0, builtin-modules 3.3.0, esbuild 0.19.0, obsidian ^1.5.0, tslib 2.6.0, typescript ^5.3.0. No production dependencies — the plugin is built with esbuild + TypeScript targeting the Obsidian API. 21 lines.
nodes.languageNotes: Standard npm package.json. Build relies on esbuild (a fast JS bundler) and TypeScript compiler (tsc). The obsidian devDependency at ^1.5.0 provides Obsidian API type definitions. No runtime dependencies — the plugin bundles directly into main.js.

metadata.fileStatusSummary: All 3 files exist on disk. The obsidian-mnemosyne integration is a minimal v0.1.0 plugin with documentation (README.md), plugin manifest (manifest.json), and build config (package.json). A companion source file (main.ts) exists in the same directory but is not part of this batch.
